methods.method_name: Zircon U-Pb Age Analysis using SIMS
methods.description: Process for determining the age of zircon samples using Secondary Ion Mass Spectrometry (SIMS) by analyzing U-Pb isotopic ratios. Analyses were conducted at IGGCAS and SKLaBIG GIGCAS.

steps.description: Zircon U-Pb analyses for sample 5133-1 and samples 11WL59-2 and 11WL60-3 were conducted using the Cameca IMS-1280 SIMS (CASIMS) at the Institute of Geology and Geophysics, Chinese Academy of Sciences (IGGCAS) and the State Key Laboratory of Isotope Geochemistry, Guangzhou Institute of Geochemistry, Chinese Academy of Sciences (SKLaBIG GIGCAS), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform SIMS analysis on zircon samples for U-Pb dating.
outputs: Raw secondary ion beam intensity data

steps.description: The O 2 À primary ion beam with an intensity of ca. 10 nA was accelerated at À 13 kV.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure SIMS primary ion beam parameters.
inputs: O2- primary beam settings (intensity, acceleration voltage)
outputs: Configured SIMS instrument

steps.description: The ellipsoidal spot is about 20 Â 30 mm in size. The aperture illumination mode (Kohler illumination) was used with a 200 mm primary beam mass filter aperture to produce even sputtering over the entire analysed area.
steps.searchable_summary: Set SIMS analysis spot size and illumination mode.
outputs: Configured SIMS instrument

steps.description: In the secondary ion beam optics, a 60 eV energy window was used, together with a mass resolution of ca. 5,400. Rectangular lenses were activated in the secondary ion optics to increase the transmission at high-mass resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure SIMS secondary ion beam optics and mass resolution.
outputs: Configured SIMS instrument

steps.description: A single electron multiplier was used on ion-counting mode to measure secondary ion beam intensities by peak jumping sequence: 196 ( 90 Zr 2  .24 s, 2.08 s, 1.04 s, 2.08 s, 2.08 s, 2.08 s and 0.24 s, respectively. Each measurement consisted of seven cycles, and the total analytical time is ca. 12 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure secondary ion beam intensities using peak jumping sequence on SIMS.
inputs: Peak jumping sequence (list of isotopes/masses and dwell times)
outputs: Raw ion count data for specified isotopes/masses

steps.description: Calibration of Pb/U ratios is relative to the zircon standard TEMORA 2 (417 Myr ago) based on an observed linear relationship between ln( 206 Pb/ 238 U) and ln( 238 U 16 O 2 / 238 U).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate Pb/U ratios using TEMORA 2 standard and linear regression.
inputs: Raw ion count data for TEMORA 2
Raw ion count data for samples
Observed linear relationship model (ln(206Pb/238U) vs ln(238U16O2/238U))
outputs: Calibrated Pb/U ratios for samples

steps.description: U and Th concentrations of unknowns were determined relative to the standard zircon 91500 (1,065 Myr ago) with Th and U concentrations of ca. 29 p.p.m. and 81 p.p.m., respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine U and Th concentrations using 91500 standard.
inputs: 91500 U/Th concentrations (29 ppm Th, 81 ppm U)
Raw ion count data for samples
outputs: U and Th concentrations for samples

steps.description: Measured compositions were corrected for common Pb using non-radiogenic 204 Pb.
steps.searchable_summary: Correct measured isotopic compositions for common lead using 204Pb.
inputs: Common Pb correction model (e.g., based on 204Pb)
outputs: Common Pb corrected isotopic compositions

steps.description: Uncertainties on individual analyses are reported at 1s level; mean ages for pooled U-Pb analyses are quoted at 95% confidence.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report uncertainties (1s) and mean ages (95% confidence) for U-Pb data.
outputs: Uncertainties (1s)
Mean ages (95% confidence interval)

methods.description: Analytical procedures used to determine the major element composition of individual minerals, and the major, trace, and Sr-Nd isotopic compositions of whole-rock samples. Analyses were carried on at the SKLaBIG GIGCAS.

steps.description: Major elements were measured on individual minerals using a JEOL JXA-8100 Superprobe with an accelerating potential of 15 kV and sample current of 20 nA.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure major element composition of individual minerals using EPMA.
outputs: Major element concentrations (wt.%) for individual minerals

steps.description: Whole-rock major element oxides (wt.%) for whole-rock powders were determined using a Varian Vista PRO ICP-AES using wavelength X-ray fluorescence spectrometry with analytical errors better than 2%.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine whole-rock major element oxides using ICP-AES or XRF.
inputs: Varian Vista PRO ICP-AES or XRF instrument
outputs: Whole-rock major element oxide concentrations (wt.%)

steps.description: Whole-rock trace elements, including the REEs, were analysed using a Perkin-Elmer ELAN 6000 inductively-coupled plasma source mass spectrometer (ICP-MS). Analytical precision for most elements is better than 3%.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze whole-rock trace elements and REEs using ICP-MS.
outputs: Whole-rock trace element and REE concentrations (ppm, ppb)

steps.description: Whole-rock Sr and Nd isotopic compositions of selected samples were determined using a Micromass Isoprobe multi-collector mass spectrometer (MC-ICP-MS).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine whole-rock Sr and Nd isotopic compositions using MC-ICP-MS.
outputs: Raw Sr and Nd isotopic ratios (e.g., 87Sr/86Sr, 143Nd/144Nd)

steps.description: The 87 Sr/ 86 Sr ratio of the NBS987 standard and 143 Nd/ 144 Nd ratio of the Shin Etsu JNdi-1 standard measured were 0.710288 ± 28 (2s) and 0.512109±12 (2s), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure standard isotopic ratios for Sr (NBS987) and Nd (JNdi-1) using MC-ICP-MS.
outputs: Measured standard isotopic ratios and uncertainties

steps.description: All measured 143 Nd/ 144 Nd and 86 Sr/ 88 Sr ratios are fractionation corrected to 146 Nd/ 144 Nd ¼ 0.7219 and 86 Sr/ 88 Sr ¼ 0.1194, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply mass fractionation correction to measured Sr and Nd isotopic ratios.
inputs: Measured Sr and Nd isotopic ratios
outputs: Mass fractionation corrected Sr and Nd isotopic ratios

methods.description: Calculation of the degree of partial melting for crust-derived magmas using simple batch melting models, constrained by isotopic and trace element data.

steps.description: These are constrained by comparing estimates of Rb/Sr in the source from combined Nd and Sr isotope ratios...
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate source Rb/Sr ratio using combined Nd and Sr isotopic data.
outputs: Estimated Rb/Sr ratio of the source

steps.description: ...with the Rb/Sr ratios of the rocks analysed...
steps.searchable_summary: Compare estimated source Rb/Sr ratio with measured whole-rock Rb/Sr ratios.
outputs: Comparison results (e.g., difference, ratio)

steps.description: ...and the REE contents of the felsic magmatic lavas together with appropriate distribution coefficients for the different minerals.
steps.searchable_summary: Incorporate REE contents of lavas and mineral-melt distribution coefficients into melting model.
inputs: Whole-rock REE concentrations for felsic lavas
Mineral-melt distribution coefficients for relevant minerals
outputs: Parameters for melting model based on REE

steps.description: The calculated degrees of partial melting for the crust-derived magmas are based on simple batch melting models. The detailed methods and equations are presented in Supplementary Methods.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate partial melting degrees using simple batch melting models.
inputs: Batch melting model equations (from Supplementary Methods)
outputs: Calculated degrees of partial melting

steps.description: The detailed methods and equations are presented in Supplementary Methods.
steps.searchable_summary: Consult supplementary materials for detailed partial melting model equations.
